titlestmt.author: Series I. compiled by Phillip Shapiro. Series II. complied by RMC staff.

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-08-29 00:15:45 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)
Re-arrangement of the collection. Addition Series II. Update Abstract, Scope and Content, and Conditions Governing Use.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: The Bound for Glory audio recordings capture the history of the folk music show created and hosted by Phil Shapiro (Cornell M.A. '69), featuring a diverse array of folk performers from 1969 to 2015. The collection includes tapes and CDs with handwritten notes by Shapiro, detailing the performances and listing the songs played.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp: //rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Bound for Glory is North America's longest running live folk concert radio broadcast. The show is broadcast live on WVBR 93.5 FM from Anabel Taylor Hall on the Cornell University campus Sunday nights. While the name of the room where the show is broadcast has changed over the years, the Commons Coffeehouse, Cul de Snack, the Cafe at Anabel Taylor, Alternatives Library, the space itself in Anabel Taylor has remained constant. Phil Shapiro made his first Ithaca broadcast of the show in September 1967 on the WVBR radio station. Bound for Glory presents folk music of all types and has run for over 1,500 concerts.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: The Bound for Glory Records document the history of the folk music show "Bound for Glory," created and hosted by Phil Shapiro (Cornell M.A. '69), spanning from 1969 to 2015. The collection includes over 1,500 audio recordings featuring a diverse array of folk performers. Series I. Audio Tapes contains reels with handwritten notes by Shapiro, detailing the performances and listing the songs played. These notes may contain spelling errors and song titles based on phonetic interpretations rather than official titles. Notable songs are often marked. This series also includes digital recordings created by Doug Steinfeld from tapes collected by himself, Sari Goldbaum, and Janet Elis, identified as part of the Doug Steinfeld Collection. Series II. CDs covers shows from 2004 to 2015 and has been digitized for preservation and access. Series III. Digital Material includes a crawled version of the show's website, a documentary by Brad Kolodner, and a digital tribute book commemorating the 1,500th show.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: The Bound for Glory Documentary is available online here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iF3N2xA5RB8

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: A portion of recordings in Series I. come from the collection of Doug Steinfeld. These tapes were digitized by Steinfeld who remains in possession of the physical tapes. Some of these recordings have a break from the initial recording when the tape was flipped over during set two of the performance. The Steinfeld collection also contains a set of Encore tapes. These tapes contain encores for shows that were not necessarily included on the on air broadcast or on the show tape.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. In Series I., audio reels TR-15957-TR-15976, TR-17143, and TR-17144 have been digitized as well as the track listing inserts in each of these reel cases. In Series II. CDs, all material has been digitized (CD7010-CD7129, CD132-CD7400,CD7406-CD7573).

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Bound for Glory records, #35-9-4277. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shows were recorded on open reel audio, pre-1998, digital audio tape, apx. 1998-06-08 - 2006-12-03 and CDs post 2007.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Track list: Arrival; I Don't Amount to Much Without You; Mary, Martha and John; August Circumstances; Empty Space; While the Getting is Good; Volcanos; Twelve Turns to Midnight; Black; Here I Am Again; Peggy [unreadable]; Halfway Up the Stairs

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: These tapes were collected and digitized by Doug Steinfeld, but not all of them were necessarily recorded by him originally. Some of these recordings have a break from the initial recording when the tape was flipped over during set two of the performance.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: All tapes have been digitized and are available upon request.

Bound for Glory 1500th Show Phil Shapiro Tribute Book (digital)

dao.xlink:title: Bound for Glory 1500th Show Phil Shapiro Tribute Book [Preservation]

daodesc.p: Bound for Glory 1500th Show Phil Shapiro Tribute Book [Preservation]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: CD-2668 a digital copy of the book.

did.unittitle: Bound for Glory The Documentary by Brad Kolodner (digital)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Short documentary by Brad Kolodner and Aya Hoffman when they were Ithaca College seniors. "This is a story about the passion and love one man has for folk music and the sacrifices he's made to keep this folk treasure alive."

dao.xlink:title: Bound for Glory North America's longest-running live folk concert [Delivery]

daodesc.p: Bound for Glory North America's longest-running live folk concert [Delivery]
